notes: Auto-executed tool call: the agent invokes a tool mid-turn (a shell command), the tool auto-resolves with no user approval, and the turn completes — state never enters waiting. Codex has no permission hooks (capabilities.json permission_hooks:false), so unlike claudecode's PostToolUse-gated variant the session does NOT end in waiting; the canonical arc is ready→working→ready. Recorded with the INTERACTIVE driver, not headless `codex exec`: a single-shot `exec` turn is too short for the daemon to observe the ready→working flip (debounce coalesces it — see basic-turn, where turn_start fails 3/5), and its turn-end surfaces as process_exited rather than a working→ready settle. The interactive REPL stays alive across the turn, so the daemon observes the full working→ready arc on the live rollout session, then process_exited only at REPL teardown (same shape that lets multi-turn-conversation pass 10/10). The tool call itself is observable in transcript.jsonl as codex function_call / function_call_output events (parser.go) and the spec's contains_tool_call signal is verified there; the phase DSL has no tool-event form, so the tool call is asserted only via the working span it sustains, not a dedicated phase.

text: Pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch
Rollout session reconciles (different session_id), born ready
PID bound to the rollout session
User prompt requiring tool use → working on the rollout session
Tool auto-resolves and the turn completes → back to ready; state never entered waiting
Driver tears down the REPL — process_exited on the rollout session
